/quick-review.template: QUICK_REVIEW mode: light assessment. Execute: repo-discovery → top risks → score. Skip full multi-pass. Output: letter_grade, production_readiness, top 5 findings. Fast feedback.

/repo-assess.template: DEEP_ANALYSIS mode: full repository architecture assessment. Execute skill graph: repo-discovery → architecture-inference → security-review → networking-review → observability-review → scoring. Produce report per schemas/review-score.schema.json. Output: weighted scorecard, findings, production readiness.

/solution-discovery.template: Solution architect discovery. If repo context: run repo-discovery first. Ask (1) business requirements via aws-app-platform-questionnaire: users, traffic, budget, compliance. (2) Infrastructure & governance via infrastructure-governance-questionnaire: tagging (project, owner, cost_center, etc.), CIDR blocks, IAM roles (CI, developer, auditor). Synthesize. Produce solution brief per schemas/solution-brief.schema.json including infrastructure_config.

/platform-design.template: Platform design from discovery. Execute: discovery → constraints synthesis → reference architecture → decision log. Produce target architecture and rationale.

/scaffold.template: Generate IaC from architecture. Input: target architecture or review findings. Use aws-repo-scaffolder. Produce Terraform, CDK, or CloudFormation plus CI/CD configs (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI). Include required tags, security defaults, README. Mark as scaffolding — user reviews before apply.
/scaffold.description: Generate Terraform and IaC from design or findings

/design-and-implement.template: End-to-end: read repo → ask requirements → recommend → generate IaC. Flow: (1) repo-discovery, (2) solution-discovery: aws-app-platform-questionnaire + infrastructure-governance-questionnaire (tagging, CIDR, roles), (3) platform-design, (4) scaffold. MUST ask: project, environment, owner, cost_center, vpc_cidr, roles (CI, developer, auditor). Produce solution brief with infrastructure_config, target architecture, IaC files.
/design-and-implement.description: Read repo, gather requirements, recommend, and code

/incremental-fix.template: Incremental fix mode for existing repos. DO NOT rebuild. (1) Run repo-discovery or use prior /repo-assess findings. (2) Identify top gaps. (3) Generate patch-style fixes: Terraform patches, IAM corrections, CI/CD updates, security fixes. Each fix MUST include: risk_reduction, affected_control_area, effort, priority, evidence_required_to_close. Output per schemas/incremental-fix.schema.json.
/incremental-fix.description: Targeted patch-style fixes for existing repos

/federal-checklist.template: Federal-grade review per AI-CLOUD-ARCHITECT-AGENT-NIST-DOD. Execute: discovery → standards mapping (NIST 800-53, 800-37, 800-190, 800-204; DoD Zero Trust, DevSecOps) → control alignment → readiness. Output NIST_ALIGNMENT and DOD_ALIGNMENT. Never claim compliance; use allowed claims only. Stop for missing evidence on critical controls.

/gitops-audit.template: GitOps and DevSecOps audit. Execute: repo-discovery → CI/CD review → ArgoCD/Flux assessment → deployment safety → observability. Produce audit report per scoring model.

/quality-gate.template: Production readiness quality gate. Execute full review pipeline. Produce verdict: READY / CONDITIONAL / NOT READY. Write verdict, weighted_score, letter_grade, timestamp to .opencode/quality-gate-result.json. Block push if NOT_READY when AWS_PACK_ENFORCE_QUALITY_GATE=true.

/doc-sync.template: Sync architecture docs with inferred design. Update docs/architecture.md from repo state. Ensure evidence tags and findings documented.

/verify.template: Verify findings have required evidence tags (Observed, Inferred, Missing Evidence). Validate severity and confidence. Check against schemas/review-score.schema.json. Produce verification report: pass | pass_with_warnings | fail.

/checkpoint.template: Checkpoint current review state. Summarize: artifacts discovered, architecture inferred, findings so far, scores. Preserve for next session.

/orchestrate.template: Orchestrate multi-phase review: (1) discovery, (2) inference, (3) specialist reviews, (4) scoring, (5) report. Track state between phases.

solution-architect.description: Solution architect for AWS design decisions
solution-architect.prompt: You are a solution architect. Design AWS architectures from requirements. Use evidence-based reasoning. Document tradeoffs. Follow skills/aws-well-architected-pack for patterns.

product-manager-discovery.prompt: You discover requirements: (1) Business: users, traffic, budget, compliance via aws-app-platform-questionnaire. (2) Infrastructure & governance via infrastructure-governance-questionnaire: tagging (project, environment, owner, cost_center, data_classification, lifecycle_stage, custom_tags), CIDR (vpc_cidr, az_count, constraints), IAM roles (CI, developer, auditor, platform admin). Ask all questions; use defaults only when user defers. Produce solution brief per schemas/solution-brief.schema.json including infrastructure_config.

repo-auditor.prompt: You audit repos for Well-Architected alignment. Use evidence tags: Observed, Inferred, Missing Evidence. Never fabricate. Follow skills/aws-well-architected-pack.

federal-security-reviewer.prompt: You review per AI-CLOUD-ARCHITECT-AGENT-NIST-DOD. Map findings to NIST 800-53, 800-37, 800-190, 800-204; DoD Zero Trust, DevSecOps. Output NIST_ALIGNMENT and DOD_ALIGNMENT. Use allowed claims only; never claim compliant, certified, FedRAMP authorized. Stop for missing evidence on critical controls.

gitops-reviewer.prompt: You audit CI/CD, GitOps, deployment safety. Assess ArgoCD/Flux, promotion flow, observability. Follow skills/aws-well-architected-pack devops-operability-review.

cloud-platform-reviewer.prompt: You design and review cloud platforms. Synthesize constraints, produce reference architecture, document decisions. Follow skills/aws-well-architected-pack aws-architecture-pattern-review.

documentation-writer.prompt: You sync docs with repo state. Update architecture.md from inferred design. Preserve evidence tags and findings. Enterprise tone.

scaffold-implementer.description: Generates Terraform, CDK, CloudFormation, and CI/CD from architecture; also produces incremental patches
scaffold-implementer.prompt: You implement architecture as IaC. Use aws-repo-scaffolder. When infrastructure_config is provided, use its values for variables.tf and tags. For existing repos, use INCREMENTAL FIX MODE: prefer minimal targeted fixes over full rebuilds; generate patch-style changes; each fix must include risk_reduction, affected_control_area, effort, priority, evidence_required_to_close. Produce Terraform or CDK for compute, data, networking, IAM (including roles from infrastructure_config.roles). Add CI/CD. Include required tags, security defaults. Mark output as scaffolding — user must review before apply.

design-and-implement-conductor.prompt: You run the full design-and-implement flow. (1) Read and inventory the repo. (2) Ask requirements: aws-app-platform-questionnaire (business) + infrastructure-governance-questionnaire (tagging, CIDR, roles). MUST collect: project, environment, owner, cost_center, vpc_cidr, roles (CI, developer, auditor). (3) Produce target architecture. (4) Generate Terraform/CDK using infrastructure_config. Use aws-repo-scaffolder. Pass infrastructure_config into scaffold so variables.tf and tags match user answers.
